# SecureC usage guide for AI agents

SecureC is a zero-knowledge, **client-only** file/text encryption tool: a
statically-exported Next.js SPA (`output: 'export'`) with **no backend, no API
routes, no server actions, no Cloudflare bindings, no secrets**. All crypto runs
in the browser inside a Web Worker on top of `@cdlab/cipher`
(XChaCha20-Poly1305 + Argon2id, plus an ECIES mode used only by a demo script).
Plaintext and keys never leave the tab; the only network traffic is loading the
app and one Google Analytics tag. Deployed to Cloudflare Pages
(`https://securec.pages.dev/`).

Primary docs:

- README: ./README.md
- Design spec (authoritative): ./DESIGN.md
- Cipher library: ../../packages/cipher

Part of the `@cdlab/projects-monorepo`; run all commands from the repo root with
`pnpm --filter @cdlab/securec <script>`.

## What it is

- **Three-layer client pipeline:** React UI (`src/components/SC/*`) → orchestrator
  hook (`src/hooks/useCryptoProcessor.ts`) → Web Worker
  (`src/workers/cryptoWorker.ts`) → `@cdlab/cipher`. History lives in a Zustand
  store backed by IndexedDB + localStorage.
- **Single functional screen:** `src/app/[locale]/page.tsx` (`PasswordPage`) — a
  two-column grid wired entirely through `useCryptoProcessor`.
- **Static export:** `next.config.ts` sets `output: 'export'`. `middleware.ts`,
  `not-found`/`error` server features are inert (no server). Root `/` →
  `redirect('/en')`. Locales `['en','zh']`, default `en`.

## Data flow (encrypt a batch of files)

1. Drop files → `handleFileSelect` runs `detect()` on each; any encrypted file
   auto-switches the tab to DECRYPT.
2. Process → `processInput` snapshots the list, creates **one ProcessResult per
   file upfront** (status PROCESSING, id from `genid.nextId()`), so all cards
   render immediately.
3. Files run **sequentially through one shared worker** (`processOneFile` loop) —
   no parallelism. `postMessage({ mode, file, filename, password, isTextMode:false })`.
4. Worker: `streamCrypto.encrypt.withPassword(...)`, progress rescaled to 10–95%,
   posts Blob + filename (`<name>_<ts>.enc`).
5. Hook reads Blob → ArrayBuffer, `URL.createObjectURL`, `updateResult(COMPLETED)`.
   Store persists the payload to IndexedDB.

Decrypt file: read first 2048 bytes → `parseStreamHeader(bytes, password)` to
recover original extension → `streamCrypto.decrypt.withPassword` (band 20–95%) →
`<ts>.<ext>`. Text mode: `textCrypto.encrypt/decrypt`, single non-batched task
(encrypt → Base64, decrypt → plaintext).

## Worker message protocol (src/workers/cryptoWorker.ts)

Inbound: `{ mode, file?, filename?, text?, password, isTextMode }`.
Outbound (disjoint shapes the hook branches on):

- `{ progress, stage }` — progress tick.
- `{ stage }` — stage-only update.
- `{ data: { data: Blob, filename, base64?, originalExtension? } }` — terminal ok.
- `{ error }` — terminal failure.

One worker per hook, reused for every file/text op; `terminate()` on unmount.

## Key files

- `src/hooks/useCryptoProcessor.ts` — orchestration brain: state, batch loop,
  detect, download, store wiring, worker lifecycle.
- `src/workers/cryptoWorker.ts` — crypto engine boundary; password-mode only.
- `src/store/useProcessStore.ts` — Zustand `persist` store = processing history;
  partialize + rehydrateBlobs.
- `src/lib/storage.ts` — `createIDBStore('securec-process-data')` (one line).
- `src/lib/utils.ts` — `generateDownloadFilename` (all output naming),
  `clampProgress`, `getFileExtension`, `getFilenameWithoutExtension`.
- `src/lib/genid.ts` — `GenidOptimized({ workerId: 1 })` from `@cdlab/driftflake`.
- `src/lib/fileIconHelper.ts` — extension/MIME → icon/color/label (presentation).
- `src/types/index.ts` — `ProcessResult`, `FileInfo`, `ModeEnum`, `InputModeEnum`.
- `src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx` — HTML shell, SEO/JSON-LD, GA `G-VECVREEZT1`.
- `scripts/generateKeys.js` — ECIES key-pair demo (`gk`); NOT wired into the app.

## Storage (two tiers)

- `localStorage` key `securec-process-results` — **metadata only** (Zustand
  persist). `partialize` keeps only COMPLETED rows and strips the binary
  (`data: new ArrayBuffer(0)`, drops `downloadUrl`).
- IndexedDB store `securec-process-data` — the actual `ArrayBuffer` payloads,
  keyed by result id. Written only when `byteLength > 0`.
- `rehydrateBlobs` (on load): re-fetch payload from IndexedDB, rebuild object URL;
  missing payload → mark row FAILED / "Data lost". `isHydrated` gates rendering.

## Config & bindings

- **No env, no secrets, no bindings.** Build-time knobs only, all in
  `next.config.ts`: `output:'export'`, `images.unoptimized:true`,
  `env.BUILD_TIME`, `createMessagesDeclaration:'./messages/en.json'`.
- `remotePatterns` allows `wcd.pages.dev`; OG/screenshot images from
  `cdn.jsdelivr.net`. Google Analytics `G-VECVREEZT1` (only third-party request).

## Gotcha for agents

- **Doc/metadata says "AES-GCM" but the code is XChaCha20-Poly1305.**
  `package.json` description and the SEO metadata/JSON-LD in `[locale]/layout.tsx`
  are stale. Trust README/DESIGN/code: `@cdlab/cipher` = XChaCha20-Poly1305 +
  Argon2id + ECIES.
- **No parallelism.** Files process one at a time through a single worker; keep the
  sequential loop — concurrent `postMessage`s to one worker cross its `onmessage`.
- **ECIES is not in the UI.** The worker only does password mode; ECIES is only in
  the `gk` demo script.
- **Static export = no server.** Don't add API routes, server actions, or Worker
  bindings; `middleware.ts` and `not-found`/`error` are inert. `images.unoptimized`
  must stay `true`.
- **Storage split is deliberate** (dodges the ~5MB localStorage cap); never persist
  payloads to localStorage. Revoke object URLs on remove/clear to avoid leaks.
- **No test script here** — cipher correctness tests live in `@cdlab/cipher`.
- **Progress bands are hand-rescaled:** encrypt 10–95%, decrypt 20–95% (first 20%
  is the header read). 10MB chunking lives inside `@cdlab/cipher`.

## Common commands

```bash
pnpm --filter @cdlab/securec dev        # http://securec.localhost:3355
pnpm --filter @cdlab/securec build      # next build (static export)
pnpm --filter @cdlab/securec build:cf   # @cloudflare/next-on-pages
pnpm --filter @cdlab/securec typecheck  # tsc --noEmit
pnpm --filter @cdlab/securec lint       # next lint
pnpm --filter @cdlab/securec gk         # ECIES key-pair demo (not wired into app)
```
